casement.def: 縱向旋放窗、框、覆蓋的人 (或物) (窗)
casement.ex: She looked out through the open casement at the garden.

casework.def: 社會工作、個案研究的人物 (單數)
casework.ex: She specializes in psychiatric casework within the hospital.

caseworker.def: 社會工作者、個案研究員 (單數)
caseworker.ex: The local caseworker visited the family on Thursday afternoon.

cash.def: 現金、現款、兌現、換錢、現金的人人物、換錢地 (現)
cash.col: cash on delivery (貨到付款), cold hard cash, cash flow (現金流), cash in on (從中獲利)
cash.ex: The store offers a discount if you pay in cash.

cash-book/cashbook.ex: The accountant spent the afternoon updating the company's cash-book.

cashed.def: 兌現了的、換成現金的人的人 (或物)
cashed.ex: He finally cashed the winning lottery ticket.

cashew.def: 腰果、腰果樹的人物 (單數)
cashew.ex: She added some roasted cashews to the spicy vegetable stir-fry.

cashier.def: 出納員、收銀員、撤職、開除的人物、撤職地 (出納)
cashier.ex: The supermarket cashier scanned the items quickly and efficiently.

cashing.def: 兌現、換成現金的人 (或物) (現)
cashing.ex: He is cashing in his retirement savings early.

cashmere.def: 喀什米爾羊絨、羊絨織物的人物 (單數)
cashmere.ex: The soft cashmere sweater was a luxury gift from her parents.

casing.def: 套、框、殼、包裝的人 (或物) (殼)
casing.ex: The industrial machine is protected by a thick steel casing.

casino.def: 賭場、俱樂部的人物 (單數)
casino.ex: They spent the entire night at the grand casino in Las Vegas.

cask.def: 大桶、一桶之量、桶裝、入桶、桶的人的人物、桶裝地 (桶)
cask.ex: The vintage wine was aged in a massive oak cask.

casket.def: 小箱、珠寶盒、(美)棺材、入箱、人的人的人物、入箱地 (箱)
casket.ex: She kept her precious necklace in a small, silver jewel casket.

caspian.def: 黑海的、裏海的人的人物 (裏海)
caspian.ex: The Caspian Sea is the world's largest inland body of water.

cassava.ex: Cassava is a major dietary staple in many tropical countries.

casserole.def: 沙鍋(菜)、燉鍋、用沙鍋煮的人的人、沙鍋煮地 (鍋)
casserole.ex: She made a delicious chicken casserole for dinner.

cassette.def: 卡帶、錄音(影)帶人物 (單數)
cassette.ex: He still has a large collection of old audio cassette tapes.

cassia.def: 肉桂(皮)、(植)山扁豆的人物 (單數)
cassia.ex: Fragrant cassia is often used as a cheaper substitute for cinnamon.

cassock.ex: The village priest wore a simple black cassock for the ceremony.

cast.def: 投、擲、拋、鑄造、計算、脫落、全體演員、投擲的人的人物、拋擲地 (投)
cast.col: cast a vote, cast a shadow, star cast (明星陣容), plaster cast (石膏繃帶)
cast.ex: The fisherman managed to cast his line deep into the lake.

castanet.ex: The dancer used the rhythm of the wooden castanets nicely.

castaway.def: 失事船隻的船員、被遺棄者、被遺棄的人的人物 (棄)
castaway.ex: The lonely castaway survived on the desert island for ten years.

caste.def: (印度)種姓制度、等級的人物 (單數)
caste.ex: The traditional caste system in India is slowly changing.

caster/castor.def: 小輪、調味瓶、投擲者、小輪子的人的人物 (輪)
caster/castor.ex: He fixed a new metal caster to the bottom of the old chair.

castigate.def: 嚴懲、責罵、批評的人的人物、責罵地 (懲)
castigate.ex: The teacher had to castigate the students for their poor behavior.

castigation.ex: The politician faced public castigation for his offensive remarks.

casting.def: 投、擲、鑄件、演員挑選的人 (或物) (鑄)
casting.ex: The director is holding a casting call for his next major film.

castle.def: 城堡、(棋)車、住在城堡中、堡的人的人物、城堡地 (堡)
castle.col: sand castle, castle in the air (白日夢), moated castle
castle.ex: The medieval castle stood on a high hill overlooking the sea.

castor/caster.def: 小輪子、蓖麻、小輪的人的人物 (輪)
castor/caster.ex: Castor oil is well-known for its very strong laxative properties.

castrate.def: 閹割、使失去活力的人的人物、閹割地 (閹)
castrate.ex: Farmers traditionally castrate young bulls to make them calmer.

castration.ex: The study examined the long-term effects of surgical castration.
